## 🔐 Security Architecture

### Authentication Flow

```
1. User Login
   ├→ POST /api/v1/auth/login
   ├→ Email + Password
   └→ Validate against bcrypt hash

2. JWT Generation
   ├→ Payload: { userId, tenantId, role, email }
   ├→ Sign with JWT_SECRET
   ├→ Expiration: 1 hour (configurable)
   └→ Return access_token

3. Request Authentication
   ├→ Client sends: Authorization: Bearer <token>
   ├→ API Gateway verifies JWT signature
   ├→ Extract user info from token
   └→ Attach to request.user

4. Token Refresh
   ├→ POST /api/v1/auth/refresh
   ├→ Send old token
   ├→ Verify and generate new token
   └→ Return new access_token
```

### Authorization (RBAC)

```
Role Hierarchy:
  super_admin (level 5)
    ├→ All permissions
    └→ System-wide access

  tenant_owner (level 4)
    ├→ Tenant management
    ├→ User management
    └→ Device management

  admin (level 3)
    ├→ Device management
    ├→ Command execution
    └→ Alert management

  operator (level 2)
    ├→ Command execution
    ├→ View devices
    └→ View alerts

  viewer (level 1)
    ├→ Read-only access
    └→ View devices & alerts
```

### Permission Checks

```typescript
// Middleware pattern
app.post('/devices/:id/command', 
  app.authenticate,           // Verify JWT
  operatorUp,                 // Check role >= operator
  validate(commandSchema),    // Validate input
  async (request, reply) => {
    // Handler
  }
);
```

### Data Encryption

```
At Rest:
  ├→ PostgreSQL: Use pgcrypto extension
  ├→ Redis: Use TLS
  └→ Secrets: Use HashiCorp Vault

In Transit:
  ├→ HTTPS/TLS 1.3
  ├→ WebSocket WSS
  └→ MQTT over TLS
```

---

## 🚀 Performance Optimization

### Caching Strategy

```
Layer 1: Redis Cache (Hot Data)
  ├→ Device online status (120s TTL)
  ├→ Command queue (persistent)
  ├→ Device shadow state (30d TTL)
  ├→ AI provider configs (24h TTL)
  └→ Rate limit counters (1m TTL)

Layer 2: PostgreSQL (Warm Data)
  ├→ Device details
  ├→ Command history
  ├→ Alert history
  └→ User data

Layer 3: ClickHouse (Cold Data)
  ├→ Telemetry (7d retention)
  ├→ Analytics
  └→ Historical reports
```

### Query Optimization

```sql
-- Device list with pagination
SELECT * FROM devices
WHERE tenantId = $1 AND status = $2
ORDER BY createdAt DESC
LIMIT $3 OFFSET $4;

-- Indexes ensure:
-- - tenantId + status filtering: O(log n)
-- - createdAt sorting: O(log n)
-- - Overall: O(log n + k) where k = limit

-- Telemetry aggregation
SELECT 
  DATE_TRUNC('minute', timestamp) as minute,
  AVG(cpuUsage) as avg_cpu,
  MAX(cpuUsage) as max_cpu,
  COUNT(*) as sample_count
FROM telemetry
WHERE deviceId = $1 AND timestamp > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('minute', timestamp)
ORDER BY minute DESC;

-- Indexes:
-- - (deviceId, timestamp) for filtering
-- - Partial index on recent data
```

### Connection Pooling

```typescript
// PostgreSQL connection pool
const pool = new Pool({
  max: 20,                    // Max connections
  idleTimeoutMillis: 30000,   // 30s idle timeout
  connectionTimeoutMillis: 2000,
});

// Redis connection pool
const redis = new Redis({
  maxRetriesPerRequest: null,
  enableReadyCheck: false,
  enableOfflineQueue: true,
  retryStrategy: (times) => Math.min(times * 50, 2000),
});
```

### Batch Operations

```typescript
// Batch device updates
const updateDevices = async (updates: Array<{ id: string; status: string }>) => {
  // Instead of N queries:
  // for (const update of updates) {
  //   await db.update(devices).set({ status: update.status }).where(eq(devices.id, update.id));
  // }
  
  // Use single query:
  const cases = updates.map(u => sql`WHEN ${u.id} THEN ${u.status}`).join(' ');
  await db.execute(sql`
    UPDATE devices 
    SET status = CASE id ${cases} END
    WHERE id IN (${updates.map(u => u.id).join(',')})
  `);
};
```

## 🔄 Scalability Patterns

### Horizontal Scaling

```
Load Balancer (Nginx/HAProxy)
    ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  API Gateway Instance 1             │
│  ├→ Redis connection pool           │
│  └→ PostgreSQL connection pool      │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
    ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  API Gateway Instance 2             │
│  ├→ Redis connection pool           │
│  └→ PostgreSQL connection pool      │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
    ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  API Gateway Instance N             │
│  ├→ Redis connection pool           │
│  └→ PostgreSQL connection pool      │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
    ↓
Shared Data Layer:
├→ PostgreSQL (primary + replicas)
├→ Redis (cluster mode)
└→ ClickHouse (distributed)
```

### Database Sharding (Future)

```
Tenant-based sharding:
  ├→ Tenant A → Shard 1 (PostgreSQL)
  ├→ Tenant B → Shard 2 (PostgreSQL)
  ├→ Tenant C → Shard 3 (PostgreSQL)
  └→ Tenant D → Shard 4 (PostgreSQL)

Routing:
  ├→ Hash(tenantId) % numShards = shardId
  └→ Connect to appropriate shard
```

### Message Queue Pattern

```
Device Commands
    ↓
Kafka Topic: device-commands
    ├→ Partition 0: Devices A-F
    ├→ Partition 1: Devices G-M
    ├→ Partition 2: Devices N-T
    └→ Partition 3: Devices U-Z
    ↓
Command Executor (4 instances)
    ├→ Instance 1 → Partition 0
    ├→ Instance 2 → Partition 1
    ├→ Instance 3 → Partition 2
    └→ Instance 4 → Partition 3
```

```
├── index.ts                 # Entry point
├── routes/                  # API routes
│   ├── auth.routes.ts
│   ├── devices.routes.ts
│   ├── orchestrator.routes.ts
│   └── ...

    ├── routes/

```

### Error Handling

```typescript
// Consistent error responses
app.setErrorHandler((error, request, reply) => {
  const statusCode = error.statusCode || 500;
  const isDev = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development';
  
  logger.error({ error, url: request.url }, 'Request error');
  
  reply.status(statusCode).send({
    success: false,
    error: error.message,
    code: error.code || 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
    ...(isDev && { stack: error.stack }),
  });
});
```

### Input Validation

```typescript
// Use Zod for schema validation
const deviceSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(1).max(255),
  type: z.enum(['android', 'linux', 'windows', ...]),
  ipAddress: z.string().ip().optional(),
  tags: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
});

app.post('/devices', 
  validate(deviceSchema),
  async (request, reply) => {
    // request.body is type-safe
  }
);
```

### Async/Await Patterns

```typescript
// Use async/await consistently
async function executeCommand(deviceId: string, command: DeviceCommand) {
  try {
    // Validate
    const device = await db.query.devices.findFirst({
      where: eq(devices.id, deviceId),
    });
    
    if (!device) throw new Error('Device not found');
    
    // Execute
    const result = await orchestrator.dispatchCommand(deviceId, command);
    
    // Persist
    await db.insert(commands).values(result);
    
    return result;
  } catch (error) {
    logger.error({ error, deviceId }, 'Command execution failed');
    throw error;
  }
}
```

## ⚡ 5-Minute Setup

```bash
# Clone repository
```

### Task 2: Execute Command on Device

```bash
# Get device ID first
DEVICE_ID=$(curl -X GET http://localhost:3388/api/v1/devices \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  | jq -r '.data[0].id')

# Execute shell command
curl -X POST http://localhost:3388/api/v1/orchestrator/devices/$DEVICE_ID/command \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "type": "shell_execute",
    "payload": {
      "command": "ls -la /home"
    },
    "timeout": 30
  }' | jq
```

### Task 3: Bulk Command Execution

```bash
# Execute command on multiple devices
curl -X POST http://localhost:3388/api/v1/orchestrator/devices/bulk/command \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "deviceIds": ["device-1", "device-2", "device-3"],
    "command": {
      "type": "shell_execute",
      "payload": {
        "command": "uptime"
      }
    }
  }' | jq
```

### Task 4: Create a Job

```bash
# Create job to execute on multiple devices
curl -X POST http://localhost:3388/api/v1/jobs \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Update All Devices",
    "description": "Update all Linux devices to latest version",
    "deviceIds": ["device-1", "device-2", "device-3"],
    "commandType": "ota_update",
    "commandPayload": {
      "version": "2.4.1",
      "rollback": true
    }
  }' | jq
```

### Task 5: Query AI Assistant

```bash
# First, connect an AI provider
curl -X POST http://localhost:3388/api/v1/ai/providers/openai-codex/connect \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "apiKey": "sk-..."
  }' | jq

# Then query
curl -X POST http://localhost:3388/api/v1/ai/query \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "query": "Show me all devices with high CPU usage",
    "provider": "openai-codex"
  }' | jq
```

### Task 6: View Telemetry Data

```bash
# Get latest telemetry
curl -X GET http://localhost:3388/api/v1/telemetry/latest \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq

# Get aggregated stats
curl -X GET http://localhost:3388/api/v1/telemetry/aggregated \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq

# Get time-series data (last 60 minutes)
curl -X GET "http://localhost:3388/api/v1/telemetry/timeseries?minutes=60" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq
```

### Task 7: Manage Alerts

```bash
# Get all alerts
curl -X GET http://localhost:3388/api/v1/alerts \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq

# Acknowledge alert
ALERT_ID=$(curl -X GET http://localhost:3388/api/v1/alerts \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  | jq -r '.data[0].id')

curl -X PUT http://localhost:3388/api/v1/alerts/$ALERT_ID/acknowledge \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq

# Resolve alert
curl -X PUT http://localhost:3388/api/v1/alerts/$ALERT_ID/resolve \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq
```

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

### Issue: Docker containers won't start

**Symptoms:**
```
Error: Cannot connect to Docker daemon
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Make sure Docker is running
docker ps

# If not running, start Docker Desktop (macOS/Windows)
# or start Docker daemon (Linux)
sudo systemctl start docker

# Try again
docker compose up -d
```

### Issue: Database connection error

**Symptoms:**
```
Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:5433
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Check if PostgreSQL container is running
docker compose ps postgres

# If not running, start it
docker compose up -d postgres

# Wait for health check to pass
docker compose ps

# Check logs
docker compose logs postgres
```

### Issue: Port already in use

**Symptoms:**
```
Error: listen EADDRINUSE :::3388
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Find process using port 3388
lsof -i :3388

# Kill the process
kill -9 <PID>

# Or use different port
API_PORT=3389 pnpm dev
```

### Issue: pnpm install fails

**Symptoms:**
```
Error: EACCES: permission denied
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Clear pnpm cache
pnpm store prune

# Try install again
pnpm install

# If still failing, use npm
npm install
```

### Issue: API returns 401 Unauthorized

**Symptoms:**
```
{"success":false,"error":"Unauthorized"}
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Check if token is valid
echo $TOKEN

# If empty, login again
TOKEN=$(curl -X POST http://localhost:3388/api/v1/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"admin@audira.local","password":"admin123456"}' \
  | jq -r '.access_token')

# Verify token is in request
curl -X GET http://localhost:3388/api/v1/devices \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -v
```

### Issue: WebSocket connection fails

**Symptoms:**
```
WebSocket connection to 'ws://localhost:3388' failed
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Check if API gateway is running
curl http://localhost:3388/api/v1/health

# Check WebSocket endpoint
curl -i -N -H "Connection: Upgrade" -H "Upgrade: websocket" \
  http://localhost:3388

# Check firewall
sudo ufw allow 3388
```

### Issue: Database migration fails

**Symptoms:**
```
Error: Migration failed
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Reset database
pnpm --filter @orchestrator/api-gateway db:reset

# This will:
# 1. Drop all tables
# 2. Push schema
# 3. Seed data

# If still failing, check logs
docker compose logs postgres
```

### Issue: Out of memory

**Symptoms:**
```
JavaScript heap out of memory
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Increase Node.js memory limit
NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 pnpm dev

# Or in package.json
"dev": "NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 next dev"
```

### Issue: Slow API responses

**Symptoms:**
```
API takes > 1 second to respond
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Check database performance
docker compose exec postgres psql -U orchestrator -d orchestrator -c "\dt"

# Check Redis connection
docker compose exec redis redis-cli ping

# Check API logs
docker compose logs api-gateway

# Monitor resource usage
docker stats
```

## ❌ Error Handling

### Error Response Format

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Error message",
  "code": "ERROR_CODE"
}
```

### Common Error Codes

| Code | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `UNAUTHORIZED` | 401 | Missing or invalid authentication token |
| `FORBIDDEN` | 403 | User lacks required permissions |
| `NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Resource not found |
| `VALIDATION_ERROR` | 400 | Invalid request parameters |
| `CONFLICT` | 409 | Resource already exists |
| `RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | 429 | Too many requests |
| `INTERNAL_ERROR` | 500 | Server error |

### Example Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Device not found",
  "code": "NOT_FOUND"
}
```

## 🔄 WebSocket Events

### Connection

```javascript
const socket = io('http://localhost:3388', {
  auth: {
    token: 'your-jwt-token'
  }
});

socket.on('connect', () => {
  console.log('Connected to server');
});
```

### Device Events

```javascript
// Device connected
socket.on('device:connected', (device) => {
  console.log('Device connected:', device);
});

// Device disconnected
socket.on('device:disconnected', (deviceId) => {
  console.log('Device disconnected:', deviceId);
});

// Device status changed
socket.on('device:status_changed', (data) => {
  console.log('Device status:', data.deviceId, data.status);
});
```

### Command Events

```javascript
// Command queued
socket.on('command:queued', (command) => {
  console.log('Command queued:', command.id);
});

// Command executing
socket.on('command:executing', (command) => {
  console.log('Command executing:', command.id);
});

// Command completed
socket.on('command:completed', (command) => {
  console.log('Command result:', command.result);
});

// Command failed
socket.on('command:failed', (command) => {
  console.log('Command failed:', command.error);
});
```

### Alert Events

```javascript
// Alert created
socket.on('alert:created', (alert) => {
  console.log('New alert:', alert.title);
});

// Alert acknowledged
socket.on('alert:acknowledged', (alertId) => {
  console.log('Alert acknowledged:', alertId);
});

// Alert resolved
socket.on('alert:resolved', (alertId) => {
  console.log('Alert resolved:', alertId);
});
```

### Telemetry Events

```javascript
// Telemetry update
socket.on('telemetry:update', (data) => {
  console.log('Telemetry:', data.deviceId, data.cpuUsage);
});
```

## 📚 Rate Limiting

**Global Rate Limit:** 200 requests per minute per IP

**Headers:**
```
X-RateLimit-Limit: 200
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 195
X-RateLimit-Reset: 1621234567
```

**When Exceeded (429):**
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Rate limit exceeded",
  "code": "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
  "retryAfter": 60
}
```
